title: DORA: Change Failure Rate
description: DORA Pillar: Change Failure Rate — Measures the percentage of deployments causing failures that require remediation. Lower failure rates indicate better testing, code review, and quality practices. Includes rework rate as a supplementary signal.

list.name: environment
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT environment FROM deployments ORDER BY environment
list.label: Environment

list.name: Deployments
list.rawQuery:
SELECT d.created_at AS time, 'Deploy: ' || d.sha AS text, 'Deployments' AS tags
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_statuses ds ON ds.deployment_id = d.id
WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
  AND ds.state = 'success'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()

options.content:
# 💥 Change Failure Rate

**DORA Pillar: Stability**

## What it Measures
Change Failure Rate measures the percentage of deployments that result in a failure requiring remediation (e.g., rollback, hotfix, or patch). It is one of the two DORA *stability* metrics and reflects the quality and reliability of changes being deployed.

## Why it Matters
A high change failure rate indicates that changes are risky and frequently break production. This leads to unplanned work, reduced trust in deployments, and slower overall delivery. Low change failure rates indicate strong testing, code review, and quality practices.

## How to Interpret
| Performance Level | Failure Rate | What it Means |
|---|---|---|
| 🟢 Elite | 0–5% | Excellent quality gates, thorough testing |
| 🟡 High | 5–10% | Good practices with occasional issues |
| 🟠 Medium | 10–15% | Testing gaps or insufficient code review |
| 🔴 Low | >15% | Significant quality issues, high deployment risk |

**Rework Rate** tracks hotfix/bugfix/rollback deployments as a supplementary signal.

## How to Take Action
- **If high**: Improve test coverage, add integration tests, strengthen code review requirements
- **If rework rate is high**: Investigate root causes of hotfixes — are they from specific repos, teams, or change types?
- **If trending up**: Check for rushed deployments, reduced review standards, or new team members needing support
- **If stable/low**: Document quality practices and share across the organization

panels.title: 📊 Key Metrics — Current failure rate performance
💥 Change Failure Rate
panels.description:
DORA: Stability · Percentage of deployments resulting in failure

🟢 Elite: 0-5%  ·  🟡 High: 5-10%  ·  🟠 Medium: 10-15%  ·  🔴 Low: >15%

targets.rawSql:
WITH deployment_outcomes AS (
  SELECT d.id, d.created_at,
    (SELECT state FROM deployment_statuses WHERE deployment_id = d.id ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1) AS latest_state
  FROM deployments d
  WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
    AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE latest_state IN ('failure', 'error')) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS change_fail_rate_percent
FROM deployment_outcomes

panels.title: 🔄 Deployment Rework Rate
panels.description:
DORA: Stability · Percentage of deployments that are rework (hotfix/bugfix/rollback)

🟢 Good: <5%  ·  🟡 Fair: 5-10%  ·  🟠 Needs Attention: 10-25%  ·  🔴 Critical: >25%

targets.rawSql:
WITH classified_deployments AS (
  SELECT d.id, d.created_at,
    CASE
      WHEN EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM deployment_pull_requests dpr
        JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.id = dpr.pull_request_id
        WHERE dpr.deployment_id = d.id
        AND pr.labels @> ANY(ARRAY['[{"name":"hotfix"}]','[{"name":"bugfix"}]','[{"name":"rollback"}]']::jsonb[])
      ) THEN true
      WHEN EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM deployment_statuses ds_prev
        JOIN deployments d_prev ON ds_prev.deployment_id = d_prev.id
        WHERE d_prev.environment = d.environment
        AND ds_prev.state IN ('failure', 'error')
        AND d.created_at - d_prev.created_at < INTERVAL '24 hours'
        AND d_prev.created_at < d.created_at
      ) THEN true
      ELSE false
    END AS is_rework
  FROM deployments d
  WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
    AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_rework) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS rework_rate_percent
FROM classified_deployments

panels.title: 📊 Change Fail Rate Trend
panels.description:
DORA: Stability · Weekly change failure rate percentage
Lower is better — Elite teams stay under 5%

targets.rawSql:
WITH deployment_outcomes AS (
  SELECT d.id, d.created_at,
    (SELECT state FROM deployment_statuses WHERE deployment_id = d.id ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1) AS latest_state
  FROM deployments d
  WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
    AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', created_at) AS time,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE latest_state IN ('failure', 'error')) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS change_fail_rate_percent
FROM deployment_outcomes
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

panels.title: 📊 Rework Rate Trend
panels.description:
DORA: Stability · Weekly deployment rework rate percentage
Lower is better — tracks hotfix/bugfix/rollback deployments

targets.rawSql:
WITH classified_deployments AS (
  SELECT d.id, d.created_at,
    CASE
      WHEN EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM deployment_pull_requests dpr
        JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.id = dpr.pull_request_id
        WHERE dpr.deployment_id = d.id
        AND pr.labels @> ANY(ARRAY['[{"name":"hotfix"}]','[{"name":"bugfix"}]','[{"name":"rollback"}]']::jsonb[])
      ) THEN true
      WHEN EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM deployment_statuses ds_prev
        JOIN deployments d_prev ON ds_prev.deployment_id = d_prev.id
        WHERE d_prev.environment = d.environment
        AND ds_prev.state IN ('failure', 'error')
        AND d.created_at - d_prev.created_at < INTERVAL '24 hours'
        AND d_prev.created_at < d.created_at
      ) THEN true
      ELSE false
    END AS is_rework
  FROM deployments d
  WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
    AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
)
SELECT
  date_trunc('week', created_at) AS time,
  COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE is_rework) * 100.0 / NULLIF(COUNT(*), 0) AS rework_rate_percent
FROM classified_deployments
GROUP BY 1
ORDER BY 1

SELECT d.environment, LEFT(d.sha, 7) AS sha, d.created_at,
  u.login AS creator,
  string_agg(DISTINCT l.label_name, ', ') AS rework_labels
FROM deployments d
JOIN deployment_pull_requests dpr ON dpr.deployment_id = d.id
JOIN pull_requests pr ON pr.id = dpr.pull_request_id
CROSS JOIN LATERAL jsonb_array_elements(pr.labels) AS label_elem
CROSS JOIN LATERAL (SELECT label_elem->>'name' AS label_name) l
LEFT JOIN users u ON u.id = d.creator_user_id
WHERE d.environment = '$environment'
  AND d.created_at BETWEEN $__timeFrom() AND $__timeTo()
  AND l.label_name IN ('hotfix', 'bugfix', 'rollback')
GROUP BY d.id, d.environment, d.sha, d.created_at, u.login
ORDER BY d.created_at DESC
LIMIT 20
